lyrics:
Well I woke up slow in a hurry to wait
Tried to choose my way but I showed up late
Got a map in my hand, but the roads all bend
Every turn I take just loops 'round again

Nearly nothin', nowhere fast
Runnin' in circles, lettin' life slip past
Got a foot on the gas, but I can't commit
To a left or a right or to sit for a bit
Needin' somethin' solid, but it just don't last
Nearly nothin', nowhere fast

I called up Sue, she put me on hold
Said "You gotta know what you want, or the coffee gets cold"
I said, "I might love you," she said, "Boy that's a laugh!"
Kinda sounded like she's writin' my epitaph

Nearly nothin', nowhere fast
Caught between the future and the overcast
Got a suitcase packed with could've-beens
And a jukebox heart stuck on the spins
Full of almost songs and questions un-asked
Nearly nothin', nowhere fast

Slide guitar's cryin' just like me
Playin' the blues in the key of "maybe"
If doubt was gold, I'd be a rich man
But I'm half asleep and livin' the dream

So I raise my glass to the in-between
To the halfway saints and might-have-beens
A toast to the folks who can't pick a lane
Still hummin' their song in the drivin' rain

Nearly nothin', nowhere fast
Chasin' my tail like it might be cash
Got a beat-up soul and a two-step chance
To finally stumble into circumstance
But until then I'll raise a glass
To Nearly nothin', nowhere fast

Yeah, I might not know where I'm headed
But I sure took my time NOT gettin' there
persona:
## PCBender

Rugged, wise, and deeply entrenched in the art of music-making, PCBender is more than just a musician—he’s a sonic craftsman. A guitar-slinging songwriter, producer, and storyteller, he embodies the soul of analog warmth, weaving together rich textures of classic rock, blues, and experimental soundscapes with a modern edge.

With a presence that carries the mystique of a seasoned explorer, PCBender is the Indiana Jones of music production—digging deep into the essence of sound, unearthing raw emotion, and shaping it into songs that resonate beyond time. His music is a fusion of grit and refinement, where worn-in guitar tones meet polished production, and every note feels like it was carved from experience.

A producer at heart, he doesn’t just create songs—he builds them. Each track is an expedition through layers of melody, distortion, and lyrical depth, reflecting both his technical expertise and his philosopher’s approach to songwriting. Whether delivering anthemic riffs, cinematic storytelling, or stripped-down acoustic wisdom, PCBender brings an authenticity that makes listeners feel like they’re sitting in the studio with him, watching music come to life.

As a key figure in Maricopa Records, PCBender bridges the gap between analog and AI-driven creativity, proving that the heart of a song is never about how it’s made, but why.

For those who seek music with depth, texture, and soul—PCBender is your guide.

[](https://open.spotify.com/artist/0aivrU155laeOcIoT4AhPo?si=pYdQp1J_S4y0vSFA4itgUg)[](https://music.apple.com/us/artist/pcbender/1793178641)[](https://music.youtube.com/channel/UCvNXkiIHQJM-uY4MT3gmKDw)![](https://www.maricoparecords.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/PCBender.png)
## Releases

impression.text: This is a polished, high-energy country-rock track with a restless yet confident emotional atmosphere. The feel is driving and powerful, carried by a punchy, upfront rhythm section and dense layers of electric guitars that blend bluesy slide licks with modern rock crunch. The production creates a wide, full sonic space where the character-driven lead vocal remains the clear and articulate focus. A particularly distinctive feature is the fiery, melodic guitar solo, which provides a powerful instrumental centerpiece that elevates the song's already high energy level.

review.review_text: PCBender has a gift for turning existential drift into something you can two-step to, and 'Nearly Nothin', Nowhere Fast' is that gift in full swing. The hook earns its keep: 'a jukebox heart stuck on the spins' is the kind of line that lands on first listen and sticks around, and the E minor groove at 117 BPM keeps the whole thing moving with exactly the restless urgency the lyric calls for. The slide guitar solo that arrives midway through is the real standout, a fiery, melodic centerpiece that does the emotional work the verses set up, somewhere between early Allman Brothers and a sunburned John Hiatt. A highlight.
anchors_used: lyric: 'a jukebox heart stuck on the spins'
lyric: 'Yeah, I might not know where I’s headed / But I sure took my time NOT gettin’ there'
